title: AI-Readiness Audit v3.0
$comment: v3.0: remediation block removed from audit file. Three-file output architecture: audit (immutable scores), remediation (prioritised plan, remediation-schema.json v1.0), editorial (prose overrides). Files joined by audit_id. v2.2: added system_name, audit_date, run_id to meta; added summary to findings; added id, value_framing, impact_categories to RemediationItem; added component_count to Summary; editorial JSON schema added as companion file. v2.1: schema aligned with v2.0 cluster-based output structure. Breaking change from v1.4 flat dimensions. New in v2.1: remediation section, severity_rank on findings, cluster_summary on clusters, recommendation mandatory on all findings. v1.4 schema preserved in git history (tag v2.0).

meta.$comment: Provenance block. Everything needed to reproduce or trace the audit run.

summary.$comment: Decision-making block. Consumed by stakeholders and CI gates. Contains no raw findings, only aggregated scores and ID references.

clusters.$comment: Primary structure. Seven clusters (0 through 6), each containing its dimensions. Replaces the v1.4 flat dimensions object. Cluster keys follow the pattern {number}_{slug}.

findings.$comment: Flat canonical list. Dimensions reference finding IDs, not the other way around. Flat structure lets the codegen pipeline iterate all findings without traversing the cluster tree.

data_gaps.$comment: Explicit record of what could not be inspected. A consumer must not treat absence of findings as a clean result.

config_ref.$comment: Pointer to the external weights config. Weights are never embedded in the audit output.

version_delta.$comment: Optional. Present when a previous audit run exists. Records score changes and their causes.

schema_version.$comment: Must match the title version. Allows consumers to assert schema compatibility before parsing.

system_name.$comment: New in v2.2. Human-readable name of the design system being audited. Used as the display title in front-end dashboards.

audit_date.$comment: New in v2.2. ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the audit run. Separate from timestamp for date-level grouping and display.

run_id.$comment: New in v2.2. Machine-generated unique identifier for this audit run. Used for deduplication and cross-referencing. Recommended format: UUID v4.

audit_id.$comment: Unique run identifier. Recommended format: {target}-v{version}-{date}, e.g. 'material-ui-v2.1-2026-04-01'.

timestamp.$comment: ISO 8601 UTC. When the audit completed, not when it started.

auditor.$comment: Who or what ran the audit, e.g. 'Claude Code via Figma REST API + MCP'.

script_version.$comment: Optional. Semantic version of the audit script.

prompt_version.$comment: Optional. Version of the prompt template used.

git_tag.$comment: Optional. Git tag of the audit tool release that produced this output. New in v2.0.

target_system.$comment: Name of the design system being audited.

evidence_sources.$comment: Optional. List of data sources used in this audit run. New in v2.0.

overall_score.$comment: Weighted average of all scored dimensions, 0-100. Weights come from the file referenced by config_ref.

phase_readiness.$comment: Derived from overall_score and blocker count. Thresholds are defined in the weights config.

conditions_for_advancement.$comment: Explicit list of what must change for the system to advance. Each condition references a finding ID.

top_blockers.$comment: Up to three finding IDs with severity=blocker, ordered by impact.

blocker_count.$comment: Total number of dimension-level blockers. New in v2.0.

dimension_scores.$comment: Flat {dimension_key: score} lookup. Score is 0-100 (dimension score x 25) or null for not-assessed dimensions. Mirrors scores inside cluster entries.

cluster_scores.$comment: Flat {cluster_key: score} lookup. Each cluster score is derived from its constituent dimensions. New in v2.0.

dimensions_scored.$comment: Count of dimensions that were scored (not null). New in v2.0. Replaces the v1.4 array of slugs.

dimensions_total.$comment: Total number of dimensions in the schema. New in v2.0.

dimensions_null.$comment: Count of dimensions scored null (not assessed, typically code-only). New in v2.0.

component_count.$comment: New in v2.2. Optional. Total published components in the audited system. Used by the impact model for per-component calculations.

ClusterEntry.$comment: A cluster groups related dimensions. New in v2.0, replacing the flat dimensions object.

cluster_name.$comment: Human-readable cluster name, e.g. 'Token and Variable System'.

cluster_summary.$comment: New in v2.1. One-sentence headline summarising the cluster's state. Used in executive summaries and dashboards.

cluster_score.$comment: Weighted average of dimension scores within the cluster, 0-100. Calculated from scored dimensions only (null dimensions excluded).

dimensions.$comment: Keyed by dimension ID following the pattern {cluster_number}.{dimension_number}_{slug}, e.g. '1.1_token_implementation'.

score.$comment: 0-4 scale. Null for dimensions that cannot be assessed (e.g. code-only dimensions when no code repo is available).

score_max.$comment: Maximum possible score. 4 for standard dimensions, 2 for Tier 2 craft baseline dimensions (4.16-4.27). New in v2.0.

severity.$comment: Dimension-level severity. Null when score is null (not assessed). Derived from score thresholds and the override rule: any sub-check at 0 forces blocker.

tier.$comment: Optional. Present on Cluster 4 dimensions. Tier 1 (4.1-4.15): scored 0-4. Tier 2 (4.16-4.27): scored 0/1/2.

narrative.$comment: Prose summary of this dimension's state. Used verbatim in the Markdown report.

evidence_sources.$comment: Which data sources contributed to this dimension's score. New in v2.0.

finding_ids.$comment: IDs pointing into the top-level findings array. Empty array means no issues found.

sub_check_scores.$comment: Optional. Keyed by sub-check ID. Each value is a 0-4 integer.

library_scope.$comment: Optional. Which library this score applies to.

checks.$comment: Optional. Present only on dimensions that use programmatic pass/fail checks.

GovernanceCheck.$comment: A single programmatic check within a dimension.

detail.$comment: Optional. Specific detail about the result.

Finding.$comment: The atomic unit of the audit. Every finding must have a recommendation (mandatory in v2.1) and a severity_rank for sorting.

id.$comment: Stable unique identifier. Format: {DIMENSION_ABBREV}-{NNN}. IDs must be stable across audit versions.

dimension.$comment: Primary dimension this finding belongs to. A finding that spans two dimensions lists the most severe as primary.

severity.$comment: 'blocker' = agent output will be wrong or missing. 'warning' = agent output will be inconsistent. 'note' = does not block but reduces quality. 'pass' = informational, no action required.

severity_rank.$comment: New in v2.1. Integer for sorting findings by severity. 0=pass, 1=note, 2=warning, 3=blocker.

node_id.$comment: Optional. Figma node ID of the affected element. Null for architectural findings.

node_name.$comment: Optional. Human-readable Figma node name. Null when node_id is null.

summary.$comment: New in v2.2. One-line overview of the finding for list views and dashboards. Distinct from description which is full detail.

description.$comment: What the problem is. One to three sentences, self-contained.

evidence.$comment: Optional. The observed data supporting the finding. Verbatim where possible.

recommendation.$comment: Mandatory in v2.1. What to do to fix it. Actionable and specific. No finding without a recommendation.

contract_ref.$comment: Optional. Which contract field this finding relates to. Null for findings with no direct contract reference.

affected_components.$comment: Optional. Component names affected by this finding.

auto_fixable.$comment: Optional. True if a script could apply the fix without human judgement.

ContractRef.$comment: Points a finding at the specific contract field it relates to.

level.$comment: Contract architecture level. Null where not applicable.

path.$comment: Relative path from repo root to the contract file. Null when the contract does not exist yet.

field.$comment: Dot-notation path to the specific field within the contract. Null when the finding applies to the whole contract.

DataGap.$comment: A named gap in audit coverage.

id.$comment: Unique gap ID, e.g. 'GAP-001'. Stable across runs.

description.$comment: What could not be inspected and why.

reason.$comment: 'timeout' = MCP timed out. 'access_denied' = API or permission restriction. 'scope_excluded' = deliberately out of scope. 'not_auditable' = structurally unreadable. 'page_size' = too many nodes for MCP.

affected_components.$comment: Optional. Component or node names inside this gap.

impact.$comment: Optional. Whether the gap likely inflates or deflates a score, and why.

path.$comment: Relative path to the weights config, e.g. 'config/scoring-weights.json'.

version.$comment: Optional. Version of the weights config active when this audit ran.

previous_score.$comment: String to accommodate 'not_scored' for new dimensions.

dimensions_changed.$comment: Only dimensions where the score changed.

narrative.$comment: Optional. Prose summary of what changed and why.
